message: Design Patterns - Common solutions to recurring problems

reasoning: Abstracts data access, enables testing, centralizes data logic

content.problem: Direct database access scattered throughout codebase
content.solution: Single source of truth for data operations
when_to_use: Want to test business logic without database

example.usage: Service depends on IUserRepository, not database

reasoning: Prevents cascading failures in distributed systems

content.problem: Failing service causes timeout waits and cascading failures
content.solution: Detect failures and short-circuit requests to failing service

reasoning: Capture all changes as sequence of events, enables audit trail and time travel

content.problem: Lost history of state changes, difficult audit trail
content.solution: Store events instead of current state, rebuild state from events
when_to_use: Complex business logic with state changes

reasoning: Separate read and write models for scalability and clarity

content.problem: Same model for reads and writes leads to complexity
when_to_use: Read and write patterns differ significantly

content.solution: Sequence of local transactions with compensating actions

types.Choreography: Each service publishes events, others react

content.example: Order saga: Reserve inventory → Charge payment → Ship order (with compensations)

reasoning: Gradually replace legacy system without big-bang rewrite

content.problem: Legacy system needs replacement but can't stop business
content.solution: Incrementally replace functionality, routing traffic to new system
phases: Phase 2: Implement new features in new system

reasoning: Tailor backend APIs to specific frontend needs

content.problem: Generic API doesn't fit mobile, web, and desktop needs
content.solution: Create separate backend layer for each frontend type
when_to_use: Need to optimize for client constraints
examples: Mobile BFF: Optimized payloads, offline support
IoT BFF: Minimal data, batch updates

reasoning: Isolate resources to prevent total system failure

content.problem: One failing component consumes all resources
content.solution: Partition resources so failure is isolated
content.analogy: Ship bulkheads: one compartment floods, ship stays afloat

reasoning: Extend application functionality without changing code

content.solution: Deploy helper container alongside main service

reasoning: Application manages cache, reads from cache first

content.problem: Database queries too slow for common reads
content.solution: Check cache first, load from DB on miss, populate cache
